extra.message: User-controlled search_query from request.args.get('search', '') is concatenated directly into a SQL query via f-string: s_query = f"SELECT filename FROM files WHERE user_id = {user_id} AND filename LIKE '%{search_query}%'". The resulting query is then executed with cursor.execute(s_query) without parameterization, allowing SQL injection.

The ProfilePicture.post() endpoint accepts a picture_url from user-supplied JSON payload and passes it directly to requests.get(picture_url) with no validation, sanitization, or URL allowlisting. This allows an attacker to make the server send requests to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or arbitrary external hosts (SSRF).

The /api/v1/profile GET endpoint has the @token_required decorator commented out (lines 152-154). Any unauthenticated user can retrieve sensitive profile information (username, email, country, role, permissions, team) for any user by simply providing a user_id parameter. This is an IDOR / Broken Object Level Authorization vulnerability.

The /api/v1/delete-user/<user_id> DELETE endpoint has the @token_required decorator commented out. Any unauthenticated user can delete any user account from the database by sending a DELETE request. This is a Broken Function Level Authorization vulnerability that allows account deletion without authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The update_profile() function allows any authenticated user to set their own role and permissions via form fields. If 'role' is present in request.form (line 187-190), it uses the user-supplied value directly. Similarly, if 'permissions' is present (line 193-196), it uses the user-supplied value. An attacker can set role=admin and permissions=all to escalate privileges. The commented-out code on lines 191-192 suggests this check was intended to be removed.

The search_query variable from request.args.get('search', '') (server/routes.py line 126) is rendered in the template using the | safe filter which disables Jinja2 auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the search GET parameter, resulting in reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).

The user.country field is rendered with the | safe filter, disabling Jinja2 auto-escaping. Since country is a user-editable field (set during registration or profile update), an attacker can store malicious HTML/JavaScript in the country field which will execute when the profile page is viewed. This is stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).

The custom_hash() function uses MD5 for password hashing without salting. MD5 is cryptographically broken and provides no brute-force protection. This is used for both login verification (line 50) and user registration (line 93).

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'just_another_secret' in the source code. This key is used for Flask session signing and JWT token generation, making all sessions forgeable. The weak value is easily guessable.

The /uploads/profile_pictures/<filename> endpoint has no authentication or authorization check. Unlike the /uploads/<filename> endpoint (lines 283-304) which verifies session login and file ownership, this endpoint serves profile pictures to any unauthenticated request. While profile pictures may not be highly sensitive, this inconsistent access control pattern exposes user data.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: DEBUG is set to True. Combined with run.py line 10 (app.run(debug=debug_mode)), this enables Flask debug mode in what appears to be a production configuration. Debug mode exposes stack traces, environment variables, and the interactive debugger to users, enabling remote code execution through the Werkzeug debugger PIN mechanism.

SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY is set to False. This means session cookies are accessible to JavaScript via document.cookie, making session hijacking trivial through any XSS vulnerability in the application.

In the ProfilePicture.post() SSRF handler, exceptions are caught and their full str(e) representation is returned to the user. This can leak internal server details such as file paths, network topology, or configuration information through error messages.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The create_dummy_data.py script contains hardcoded plaintext passwords for seed users (e.g., 'super-secret', 'password123', 'password'). Although this is a data seeding script, these credentials are hashed with MD5 and inserted into the database, creating accounts with weak, known passwords in any environment where this script is run.
